title: Coin Change
description: You have an unlimited supply of coins with denominations c_1, c_2, ..., c_n. Find the minimum number of coins needed to make a total of amount S, or determine it's impossible.
input_spec: The first line contains two integers n and S (1 <= n <= 100, 1 <= S <= 10^4). The second line contains n distinct integers c_1, ..., c_n (1 <= c_i <= S).
output_spec: Print the minimum number of coins, or -1 if impossible.

routes.name: Bottom-up DP
routes.description: Build a DP table where dp[i] = minimum coins to make amount i.

observations.title: Optimal substructure
observations.description: If the optimal solution for amount S uses coin c, then the remaining S-c must also be solved optimally.

hints.nudge: If you knew the last coin used, what problem remains?
hints.question: If you use a coin of value c, what's the remaining problem? How does that relate to the original?
hints.formal: dp[s] = min over all coins c of (dp[s - c] + 1) where s - c >= 0. This is the recurrence.

observations.title: Base case and infinity
observations.description: dp[0] = 0 (zero coins for zero amount). Initialize all other dp[i] = infinity (impossible until proven otherwise).

hints.nudge: What's the simplest amount to make? How many coins does it take?
hints.question: What should dp[0] be? What should you initialize unreachable amounts to?
hints.formal: dp[0] = 0. For i > 0, dp[i] = INF initially. After filling, if dp[S] is still INF, output -1.

observations.title: Iterate amounts 1 to S
observations.description: Process amounts in order from 1 to S. For each amount, try every coin denomination.

hints.nudge: In what order should you fill the DP table?
hints.question: When computing dp[i], which smaller subproblems do you need? Are they already computed?
hints.formal: for i in 1..=S: for c in coins: if i >= c: dp[i] = min(dp[i], dp[i-c] + 1). Order ensures dp[i-c] is ready.
